title: Iron deficiency and fatigue
source: NHS Inform; South & West Devon Formulary (NHS)
text: Iron is needed to make haemoglobin, the protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen around the body. When iron runs low, less oxygen reaches the tissues, which commonly shows up as tiredness and low energy. Ferritin is the protein that stores iron, so a low ferritin reading points to depleted iron stores. Other common signs of low iron include breathlessness and pale skin. As a rough guide, a ferritin below 30 µg/L suggests low stores and below 15 µg/L indicates iron deficiency. Persistent tiredness alongside these signs is worth raising with a GP, who can arrange a blood test.
